understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete all levels of the KA59 puzzle game by placing each selectable piece onto a goal tile (+). Every goal had to be occupied simultaneously to win. The run ended due to running out of steps (step budget of 0), and I did not reach the final level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement shifted the selected piece by 3-pixel cell increments. Walls and boundaries blocked movement, though some boundaries could be passed or pushed through. Pushable objects (blocks) could be shifted by moving into them, but walls did not move. CLICK allowed switching control to another selectable piece at its exact pixel coordinates. I observed that moving into some adjacent objects would fail (position unchanged) while others allowed passing or pushing. The heuristic `movement.{direction}_blocked` indicated an adjacent object but did not always mean movement was impossible. I did not fully explore or test the limits of wall and push interactions due to step constraints.
